title: Bombay Stock Exchange

infobox.value: * [Subhasis Chaudhuri](/wiki/Subhasis_Chaudhuri "Subhasis Chaudhuri") ([chairman](/wiki/Chairman "Chairman"))[[2]](#cite_note-2)  * Sundararaman Ramamurthy  ([MD](/wiki/Managing_Director "Managing Director") and [CEO](/wiki/Chief_Executive_Officer "Chief Executive Officer"))[[3]](#cite_note-3)
* [BSE SENSEX](/wiki/BSE_SENSEX "BSE SENSEX") * BSE 100 * BSE 500 * BSE MidCap * BSE SmallCap[[6]](#cite_note-6)

(**BSE**), is an Indian [stock exchange](/wiki/Stock_exchange "Stock exchange") based in [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"). Established in 1875, it is the oldest stock exchange in Asia, and also the tenth oldest in the world. It is the [6th largest stock exchange](/wiki/List_of_stock_exchanges "List of stock exchanges") in the world by total [market capitalisation](/wiki/Market_capitalisation "Market capitalisation"), exceeding $5 trillion in May 2024.[[8]](#cite_note-WFE-8)[[9]](#cite_note-bseindia.com-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Rawal2015-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
SEBI Regulation 45(1) of Securities Contracts (Regulation) (Stock Exchanges and Clearing Corporations) Regulations, 2018 prohibits self-listing of a stock exchange in India.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

**BSE Limited**, also known as the **Bombay Stock Exchange** (**BSE**), is an Indian [stock exchange](/wiki/Stock_exchange "Stock exchange") based in [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"). Established in 1875, it is the oldest stock exchange in Asia, and also the tenth oldest in the world. It is the [6th largest stock exchange](/wiki/List_of_stock_exchanges "List of stock exchanges") in the world by total [market capitalisation](/wiki/Market_capitalisation "Market capitalisation"), exceeding $5 trillion in May 2024.[[8]](#cite_note-WFE-8)[[9]](#cite_note-bseindia.com-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Rawal2015-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bombay_Stock_Exchange&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/Bombay_Stock_Exchange_logo.svg/250px-Bombay_Stock_Exchange_logo.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Bombay_Stock_Exchange_logo.svg)

Bombay Stock Exchange logo used until June 2023

Bombay Stock Exchange was founded by a [Jain](/wiki/Jain "Jain") businessman [Premchand Roychand](/wiki/Premchand_Roychand "Premchand Roychand") in 1875.[[12]](#cite_note-12) While BSE Limited is now synonymous with [Dalal Street](/wiki/Dalal_Street "Dalal Street"), it was not always so. In the 1850s, four [Gujarati](/wiki/Gujarati_people "Gujarati people") and one [Parsi](/wiki/Parsis "Parsis") stockbroker gathered together under a [banyan](/wiki/Banyan "Banyan") tree in front of Bombay (now Mumbai) Town Hall, where Horniman Circle is now situated.[[9]](#cite_note-bseindia.com-9)[[13]](#cite_note-13) A decade later, the brokers moved their location to under the banyan trees at the junction of Meadows Street and what was then called Esplanade Road, now MG Road. With a rapid increase in the number of brokers, they had to shift places repeatedly. At last, in 1874, the brokers found a permanent location, the one that they could call their own. The brokers group became an official organisation known as "The Native Share & Stock Brokers Association" in 1875.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In 1956, the BSE became the first stock exchange to be recognised by the [Indian Government](/wiki/Second_Nehru_ministry "Second Nehru ministry") under the Securities Contracts Regulation Act.[[15]](#cite_note-q723-15)

The BSE is also a Partner Exchange of the [United Nations Sustainable Stock Exchange initiative](/wiki/Sustainable_Stock_Exchanges_Initiative "Sustainable Stock Exchanges Initiative"), joining in September 2012.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

BSE established the [India International Exchange](/wiki/India_International_Exchange "India International Exchange") (India INX) on 30th December 2016. India INX, based out of [GIFT City](/wiki/GIFT_City "GIFT City"), is the first international stock exchange of India.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

BSE became the first stock exchange in the country to launch commodity derivatives contract in gold and silver in October 2018.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

BSE was [demutualised](/wiki/Demutualised "Demutualised") and corporatised on 19 May 2007, pursuant to the *BSE (Corporatisation and Demutualisation) Scheme, 2005* notified by [SEBI](/wiki/Securities_and_Exchange_Board_of_India "Securities and Exchange Board of India").[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)

BSE Limited listed on the [National Stock Exchange](/wiki/National_Stock_Exchange_of_India "National Stock Exchange of India") (NSE) on 3 February 2017 under the [ticker symbol](/wiki/Ticker_symbol "Ticker symbol") of "BSE".[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[a]](#cite_note-24)

## Criticism and controversies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bombay_Stock_Exchange&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Criticism and controversies")]

See also: [1992 Indian stock market scam](/wiki/1992_Indian_stock_market_scam "1992 Indian stock market scam")

BSE has witnessed several high-profile market manipulation scandals.[[24]](#cite_note-25)[[25]](#cite_note-26)[[26]](#cite_note-27)[[27]](#cite_note-28)[[28]](#cite_note-29) At times, the [Securities and Exchange Board of India](/wiki/Securities_and_Exchange_Board_of_India "Securities and Exchange Board of India") (SEBI) has barred several individuals and entities from trading on the exchanges for [insider trading](/wiki/Insider_trading "Insider trading") and [stock manipulation](/wiki/Market_manipulation "Market manipulation"), especially in [illiquid](/wiki/Market_liquidity "Market liquidity") [small-caps](/wiki/Small_cap_company "Small cap company") and [penny stocks](/wiki/Penny_stocks "Penny stocks").[[29]](#cite_note-30)[[30]](#cite_note-31)[[31]](#cite_note-32)[[32]](#cite_note-33)

The BSE faced criticism during the early 2000s for delayed technology upgrades, which contributed to its competitor NSE gaining market dominance in [electronic trading](/wiki/Electronic_trading "Electronic trading"). In 2013, market participants raised concerns over alleged preferential access to BSE's trading systems, prompting calls for improved transparency and oversight, though no regulatory action followed.[[33]](#cite_note-34)
